report_name: ‘FortiBleed’ Campaign: Over 70,000 Fortinet Firewalls Compromised in Global Credential Heist

attack_patterns.name: Brute Force
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors conducted over 1.16 billion automated login attempts, including brute force and credential stuffing, against internet-facing FortiGate firewalls and SSL-VPN gateways.

attack_patterns.name: Password Cracking
attack_patterns.description: Attackers exfiltrated SSL VPN authentication hashes (sslvpn_websession) and cracked them offline using a 45-GPU cluster managed by Hashtopolis.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The campaign's primary goal was to acquire and use valid administrative and SSL-VPN accounts to gain initial access to corporate networks.

attack_patterns.name: Domain Account
attack_patterns.description: After gaining initial access via compromised FortiGate devices, attackers pivoted into internal networks to compromise Active Directory domain accounts.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: Attackers stole configuration files, including the 'sslvpn_websession' file containing hashed credentials, from compromised FortiGate devices.

tools.name: Hashtopolis
tools.description: Used by the attackers to manage a 45-GPU cluster for the offline cracking of exfiltrated password hashes.

campaigns.name: FortiBleed
campaigns.description: A large-scale, automated credential harvesting campaign attributed to a Russian-speaking threat group, targeting Fortinet FortiGate devices worldwide. The campaign involved brute-force attacks, exfiltration of configuration files, and offline password hash cracking to compromise over 70,000 devices.

infrastructure.name: Offline Hash Cracking Cluster
infrastructure.description: A 45-GPU cluster managed by Hashtopolis, used by the attackers to crack exfiltrated SSL VPN password hashes offline.

indicators.name: FortiBleed Configuration File Exfiltration
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: FortiBleed Configuration File Access
id: 2b8f1c9a-7e3d-4b5f-8c9a-1d2e3f4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: Detects access to or creation of the 'sslvpn_websession' file, which was exfiltrated in the FortiBleed campaign to harvest credentials.
references:
  - https://www.fortinet.com/lat/blog/psirt-blogs/analysis-of-reported-credential-compromise-of-fortigate-devices
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-19
tags:
  - attack.t1005
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains: 'sslvpn_websession'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence of the 'sslvpn_websession' configuration file, which contains hashed credentials and was targeted for exfiltration in the FortiBleed campaign.

indicators.name: FortiGate Brute Force or Credential Stuffing Attempt
indicators.pattern:
title: FortiGate Brute Force or Credential Stuffing Attempt
id: 9e8d7c6b-5a4b-4c3d-8b2a-1f2e3d4c5b6a
status: experimental
description: Detects a high volume of failed login attempts against FortiGate devices, indicative of the FortiBleed campaign's brute-force and credential stuffing activities.
references:
  - https://www.fortinet.com/lat/blog/psirt-blogs/analysis-of-reported-credential-compromise-of-fortigate-devices
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-19
tags:
  - attack.t1110
level: medium
logsource:
  product: fortinet
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    keywords:
      - 'action=ssl-login-fail'
      - 'action=login-failed-for-user'
  condition: 1 of selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate users with incorrect passwords.
  - Correlation with source IP and login frequency is recommended to reduce false positives.
indicators.description: Detects log events indicating failed logins on FortiGate devices, which was a primary method used in the FortiBleed campaign to brute-force credentials.

relationships.source_name: FortiGate Brute Force or Credential Stuffing Attempt
